_about: Source facts braindb extracted from news/filings. An LLM turned each into one typed relation (the `relation` field). This step is offline here but COULD BE LIVE (an LLM call per new fact as braindb ingests it).

facts.fact: Chevron signed a 20-year power-purchase agreement to fuel a Microsoft gas-powered AI data center in West Texas.
Google Cloud announced a multi-year partnership with HSBC on 2026-06-17 to deploy AI across HSBC's global operations.
Google, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta are collectively investing ~$750bn in AI infrastructure this year.
BioArctic AB entered a research & collaboration agreement with Eli Lilly and Company.
Market analysts list Nvidia, Alphabet and Redwire as investment alternatives to SpaceX for AI/space exposure.
Smart Sand (SND) produces silica sand used as proppants for hydraulic fracturing in oil & gas drilling.
Oil & gas drilling produces the natural gas increasingly used to power AI data centers (e.g. the Chevron-Microsoft deal).
Smart Sand faces structural headwinds from in-basin sand competition, compressing margins from $21 to ~$13-15/ton.
